_comment: Cross-language parity cases for the PR[12] biaxial section-stress convention: sigma = N/A - My*y/Iy + Mz*z/Iz (y=depth, z=width); depth shear from Vz over Iy, width shear from Vy over Iz. Asserted by BOTH web/src/lib/engine/__tests__/section-stress-parity.test.ts (TS) and engine/tests/.../stress_3d.rs (Rust) so the two implementations cannot silently drift. Section is a 0.1x0.2 m rectangle: A=0.02, Iy=b*h^3/12=6.66667e-5 (depth/strong), Iz=h*b^3/12=1.66667e-5 (width/weak). Forces in kN / kN*m, lengths in m, stresses in MPa.

cases.name: pure My bends over the depth (uses Iy, negative sign at top fiber)
pure Mz bends over the width (uses Iz, positive at z>0)
combined N + My + Mz at the +y/+z corner
Vz produces depth shear, parabolic max at the neutral axis (1.5 V/A)
Vy produces width shear, parabolic max at the neutral axis (1.5 V/A)
